IsA: It is a medieval Georgian encaustic icon.
It is a holy tile imprinted with the face of Jesus Christ.
It is a relic of Georgian Christianity.
HasA: It has a chased silver riza.
It has a face of Jesus Christ miraculously transferred by contact with the Image of Edessa.
It has a face of Christ Pantocrator.
It has the bust of Jesus.
It has the scene of deesis.
It has archangels Michael and Gabriel.
It has apostles Peter and John.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being a "holy tile" imprinted with the face of Jesus Christ.
It is characterized by its medieval Georgian origin.
It is characterized by its encaustic technique.
It is characterized by a chased silver riza.
It is characterized by being partly repainted in the following centuries.
It is characterized by its association with the Image of Edessa.
It is characterized by its conversion into a triptych.
It is characterized by its gilded chasing by Beka Opizari.
It is characterized by its dimensions of 105X71X4.6 cm without a kiot.
UsedFor: It is used for veneration as one of the most venerated relics of Georgian Christianity.
It is used for displaying the face of the Savior.
It is used for depicting Christ Pantocrator.
It is used for depicting the bust of Jesus.
It is used for creating the scene of deesis.
It is used for depicting archangels Michael and Gabriel.
It is used for depicting apostles Peter and John.
It is used for depicting 12 scenes from the New Testament.
LocatedIn: It is located at the National Art Museum of Georgia in Tbilisi.
It is located in the National Art Museum of Georgia.
BuiltIn: It was built in the 6th-7th century.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the Georgian monastery of Ancha.
It originated in what is now Turkey.
It originated in the Georgian princedom of Klarjeti.
CreatedBy: It was created by unknown hands.
It was created by the contact with the Image of Edessa.
CreatedIn: It was created in the 6th-7th century.
CausedBy: It is caused by contact with the Image of Edessa.
It is caused by the iconoclastic campaign by the Byzantine emperor Leo III the Isaurian.
ConsistsOf: It consists of an encaustic painting.
It consists of a chased silver riza.
It consists of a triptych.
It consists of gilded chasing by Beka Opizari.
It consists of lateral leaves depicting 12 scenes from the New Testament.
It consists of Christ Pantocrator.
It consists of the bust of Jesus.
It consists of John the Baptist.
It consists of Mary.
It consists of archangels Michael and Gabriel.
It consists of apostles Peter and John.
PartOf: It is part of the National Art Museum of Georgia.
It is part of a triptych.
It is part of the scene of deesis.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the National Art Museum of Georgia.
KnownFor: It is known for being the Keramidion, a "holy tile" imprinted with the face of Jesus Christ.
It is known for its miraculous transfer of Jesus Christ's face by contact with the Image of Edessa.
It is known for its origin from the Georgian monastery of Ancha.
It is known for its historical significance in Georgian Christianity.
It is known for its artistic features including encaustic painting and gilded chasing.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Image of Edessa.
It is associated with the Mandylion.
It is associated with Apostle Andrew.
It is associated with the Byzantine emperor Leo III the Isaurian.
It is associated with the Georgian monastery of Ancha.
It is associated with the Georgian princedom of Klarjeti.
It is associated with the merchant Amirjan Ievangulishvili.
It is associated with the Georgian catholicos Domenti III.
It is associated with the church of the Nativity of the Theotokos in Tbilisi.
It is associated with the Anchiskhati church.
NamedAfter: It is named after the Georgian monastery of Ancha.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the following centuries.
It occurred in the early 14th century.
HeldIn: It was held in the Georgian monastery of Ancha.
It was held in the church of the Nativity of the Theotokos in Tbilisi.
It was held in the National Art Museum of Georgia.
About: It is about the face of Jesus Christ.
It is about the Image of Edessa.
It is about 12 scenes from the New Testament.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Image of Edessa.
It was influenced by the Mandylion.
It was influenced by the iconoclastic campaign by the Byzantine emperor Leo III the Isaurian.
It was influenced by the Ottoman conquest of the Georgian Principality of Samtskhe.
It was influenced by the Soviet takeover of Georgia.
Influenced: It influenced the naming of the Anchiskhati church.
